themeWords.meaning: unfaithfulness; act of treachery or violation of covenant

sentenceTheme: Ezra mourns Israel’s unfaithfulness through intermarriage and prays a humble confession, pleading for mercy upon the remnant.

verses.text: Now when these things were done, the princes came to me, saying, The people of Israel, and the priests, and the Levites, have not separated themselves from the people of the lands, doing according to their abominations, even of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Jebusites, the Ammonites, the Moabites, the Egyptians, and the Amorites.

highlightedWords.meaning: Israel; the name of the covenant people and their land
detestable things; idolatrous or ritually offensive practices

verses.text: For they have taken of their daughters for themselves, and for their sons: so that the holy seed have mingled themselves with the people of those lands: yea, the hand of the princes and rulers hath been chief in this trespass.

highlightedWords.meaning: holiness; that which is sacred or set apart
unfaithfulness; breach of trust against God

verses.text: And when I heard this thing, I rent my garment and my mantle, and plucked off the hair of my head and of my beard, and sat down astonied.

highlightedWords.meaning: garment; clothing; covering
to be appalled; desolated; astonished

verses.text: Then were assembled unto me every one that trembled at the words of the God of Israel, because of the transgression of those that had been carried away; and I sat astonied until the evening sacrifice.

highlightedWords.meaning: to tremble, quake, or fear greatly
a sacrifice; an offering slain before God

verses.text: And at the evening sacrifice I arose up from my heaviness; and having rent my garment and my mantle, I fell upon my knees, and spread out my hands unto the LORD my God,

highlightedWords.meaning: knee; joint used for kneeling in prayer or supplication
the personal name of the God of Israel

verses.text: And said, O my God, I am ashamed and blush to lift up my face to thee, my God: for our iniquities are increased over our head, and our trespass is grown up unto the heavens.

highlightedWords.meaning: to be ashamed; confounded; disappointed
iniquity; guilt; the consequence of perversity

verses.text: Since the days of our fathers have we been in a great trespass unto this day; and for our iniquities have we, our kings, and our priests, been delivered into the hand of the kings of the lands, to the sword, to captivity, and to a spoil, and to confusion of face, as it is this day.

highlightedWords.meaning: sword; instrument of war or judgment
captivity; exile; prisoners

verses.text: And now for a little space grace hath been shewed from the LORD our God, to leave us a remnant to escape, and to give us a nail in his holy place, that our God may lighten our eyes, and give us a little reviving in our bondage.

highlightedWords.meaning: the remainder; survivors preserved by God
holiness; sacred place or thing

verses.text: For we were bondmen; yet our God hath not forsaken us in our bondage, but hath extended mercy unto us in the sight of the kings of Persia, to give us a reviving, to set up the house of our God, and to repair the desolations thereof, and to give us a wall in Judah and in Jerusalem.

highlightedWords.meaning: servant; slave; bondman
Persia; the Persian empire/people

verses.text: And now, O our God, what shall we say after this? for we have forsaken thy commandments,

highlightedWords.meaning: to leave, abandon, or forsake
commandment; authoritative directive

verses.text: Which thou hast commanded by thy servants the prophets, saying, The land, unto which ye go to possess it, is an unclean land with the filthiness of the people of the lands, with their abominations, which have filled it from one end to another with their uncleanness.

highlightedWords.meaning: unclean; defiled; ritually impure
detestable things; idolatrous or ritually offensive practices

verses.text: Now therefore give not your daughters unto their sons, neither take their daughters unto your sons, nor seek their peace or their wealth for ever: that ye may be strong, and eat the good of the land, and leave it for an inheritance to your children for ever.

highlightedWords.meaning: daughter; female child
inheritance; possession; heritage

verses.text: And after all that is come upon us for our evil deeds, and for our great trespass, seeing that thou our God hast punished us less than our iniquities deserve, and hast given us such deliverance as this;

highlightedWords.meaning: iniquity; guilt; the consequence of perversity
deliverance; escape; preservation

verses.text: Should we again break thy commandments, and join in affinity with the people of these abominations? wouldest not thou be angry with us till thou hadst consumed us, so that there should be no remnant nor escaping?

highlightedWords.meaning: commandment; authoritative directive
the remainder; survivors preserved by God

verses.text: O LORD God of Israel, thou art righteous: for we remain yet escaped, as it is this day: behold, we are before thee in our trespasses: for we cannot stand before thee because of this.

highlightedWords.meaning: righteous; just; in the right
unfaithfulness; breach of trust against God
